rating.coverage_remarks: Content covers basic introduction and definitions of C, A, P but is incomplete (cuts off mid-sentence). Missing: theorem proof/explanation, practical examples of CP/AP/CA systems, PACELC model extension, real-world database implementations.
rating.quality_remarks: Content is well-written and accurate with clear explanations. However, MCQ difficulty is 'easy' which is insufficient for Standard tier - should be application/analysis level. Flashcard is basic.
rating.tier_remarks: Content depth is adequate for introduction but insufficient for Standard tier (B.Tech/MSc). Expected harder MCQs (numerical, application-based), more rigorous theory with proofs, and longer length (200-250 lines).
suggestions: Add examples of real-world databases for each CAP category (e.g., Cassandra as AP, MongoDB as CP, RDBMS as CA)
Include the PACELC model as an extension to CAP theorem
Create harder MCQs requiring application and analysis (e.g., choosing appropriate system given requirements, calculating trade-offs)
Complete the incomplete section on the fundamental trade-off theorem explanation
Add diagrams showing the CAP triangle and system placement

rating.tier_remarks: Theoretical depth is adequate for Standard tier, but MCQ difficulty is too easy. Should include numerical problems, schedule analysis, or deadlock detection questions.
